questions.explanation:
{tex}\therefore \alpha+\beta{/tex} = 64, {tex}\alpha \beta{/tex} = 256
{tex}\frac{\alpha^\frac{3 }{8}}{\beta^\frac{5}{8}}+\frac{\beta^\frac{3 }{8}}{\alpha^\frac{5}{8}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\alpha+\beta}{(\alpha \beta)^\frac{5 }{8}}=\frac{64}{\left(2^{8}\right)^\frac{5}{8}}=\frac{64}{32}{/tex} = 2

questions.question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation 2x(2x +1) = 1, then $\beta$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots of the equation 2x(2x +1) = 1, then {tex}\beta{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of the given quadratic equation,
2x2 + 2x - 1 = 0 ...(i)
Then, {tex}\alpha+\beta{/tex} = {tex}- \frac {1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 1 = 2{tex}\alpha{/tex} + 2{tex}\beta{/tex}
and {tex}4 a^{2}+2 \alpha-1{/tex} = 0 [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\alpha{/tex} is root of eq. (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 \alpha^{2}+2 \alpha+2 \alpha+2 \beta=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\beta{/tex} = {tex}-2 \alpha(\alpha+1){/tex}

questions.question: The product of the roots of the equation 9x^2 - 18|x| + 5 = 0, is:
questions.question_raw: The product of the roots of the equation 9x2 - 18|x| + 5 = 0, is:
questions.explanation:
Let |x| = y then
9y2 - 18y + 5 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9y2 - 15y - 3y + 5 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (3y - 1)(3y - 5) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac {1}{3}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{5}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|x|=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} or {tex}\frac {5}{3}{/tex}
Roots are {tex}\pm \frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\pm \frac{5}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Product = {tex}\frac {25}{81}{/tex}

questions.question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are teh roots of the equation, 7x^2 - 3x - 2 = 0, then the value of $\frac{\alpha}{1-\alpha^{2}}+\frac{\beta}{1-\beta^{2}}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are teh roots of the equation, 7x2 - 3x - 2 = 0, then the value of {tex}\frac{\alpha}{1-\alpha^{2}}+\frac{\beta}{1-\beta^{2}}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of the quadratic equation
7x2 - 3x - 2 = 0
{tex}\therefore \alpha+\beta=\frac{3}{7}{/tex}, {tex}\alpha \beta=\frac{-2}{7}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{\alpha}{1-\alpha^{2}}+\frac{\beta}{1-\beta^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\alpha-\alpha \beta(\alpha+\beta)+\beta}{1-\left(\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}\right)+(\alpha \beta)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(\alpha+\beta)-\alpha \beta(\alpha+\beta)}{1-(\alpha+\beta)^{2}+2 \alpha \beta+(\alpha \beta)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac{3}{7}+\frac{2}{7} \times \frac{3}{7}}{1-\frac{9}{49}+2 \times \frac{-2}{7}+\frac{4}{49}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{27}{16}{/tex}

questions.question: Let u = $\frac{2 z+i}{z-k i}$, z = x + iy and k > 0. If the curve represented by Re(u) + Im(u) = 1 intersects the y-axis at the points P and Q where PQ = 5, then the value of k is:
questions.question_raw: Let u = {tex}\frac{2 z+i}{z-k i}{/tex}, z = x + iy and k > 0. If the curve represented by Re(u) + Im(u) = 1 intersects the y-axis at the points P and Q where PQ = 5, then the value of k is:
questions.explanation:
u = {tex}\frac{2(x+i y)+i}{(x+i y)-k i}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x+i(2 y+1)}{x+i(y-k)}{/tex}
Real part of u = Re(u) = {tex}\frac{2 x^{2}+(y-K)(2 y+1)}{x^{2}+(y-K)^{2}}{/tex}
Imaginary part of u
= Im(u) = {tex}\frac{-2 x(y-K)+x(2 y+1)}{x^{2}+(y-K)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} Re(u) + Im(u) = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x2 + 2y2 - 2Ky + y - K - 2xy + 2Kx + 2xy + x
= x2 + y2 + K2 - 2Ky
Since, the curve intersect at y-axis,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y2 + y - K(K + 1) = 0
Let y1 and y2 are roots of equations if x = 0
{tex}\because{/tex} y1 + y2 = -1
y1y2 = -(K2 + K)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (y1 - y2)2 = (1 + 4K2 + 4K)
Given PQ = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |y1 - y2| = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4K2 + 4K - 24 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} K = 2 or -3
as K > 0, {tex}\therefore{/tex} K = 2

questions.question: Let $\lambda$ $\neq$ 0 be in R. If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are roots of the equation, x^2 - x + 2$\lambda$ = 0 and $\alpha$ and $\gamma$ are the roots of the equation, 3x^2 - 10 + 27$\lambda$ = 0, then $\frac{\beta \gamma}{\lambda}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 be in R. If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are roots of the equation, x2 - x + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\gamma{/tex} are the roots of the equation, 3x2 - 10 + 27{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0, then {tex}\frac{\beta \gamma}{\lambda}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Since {tex}\alpha{/tex} is common root of x2 - x + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 and
3x2 - 10x + 27{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0
{tex}\therefore 3 \alpha^{2}-10 \alpha+27 \lambda{/tex} = 0 ...(i)
{tex}3 \alpha^{2}-3 \alpha+6 \lambda{/tex} = 0 ...(ii)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} On subtract, we get {tex}\alpha=3 {\lambda}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\alpha \beta=2 \lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3 \lambda \cdot \beta{/tex} = 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \beta=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \alpha+\beta=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3 \lambda+\frac{2}{3}=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{1}{9}{/tex} and
{tex}\alpha \gamma=9 \lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3 \lambda \cdot \gamma=9 \lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \gamma=3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{\beta \gamma}{\lambda}=18{/tex}

questions.question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation x^2 + px + 2 = 0 and $\frac{1}{\alpha}$ and $\frac{1}{\beta}$ are the roots of the equation 2x^2 + 2qx + 1 = 0, then $\left(\alpha-\frac{1}{\alpha}\right)\left(\beta-\frac{1}{\beta}\right)$$\left(\alpha+\frac{1}{\beta}\right)\left(\beta+\frac{1}{\alpha}\right)$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots of the equation x2 + px + 2 = 0 and {tex}\frac{1}{\alpha}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{\beta}{/tex} are the roots of the equation 2x2 + 2qx + 1 = 0, then {tex}\left(\alpha-\frac{1}{\alpha}\right)\left(\beta-\frac{1}{\beta}\right){/tex}{tex}\left(\alpha+\frac{1}{\beta}\right)\left(\beta+\frac{1}{\alpha}\right){/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\alpha \cdot \beta{/tex} = 2 and {tex}\alpha+\beta{/tex} = -p also {tex}\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta}{/tex} =-q
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 2q
Now {tex}\left(\alpha-\frac{1}{\alpha}\right)\left(\beta-\frac{1}{\beta}\right)\left(\alpha+\frac{1}{\beta}\right)\left(\beta+\frac{1}{\alpha}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left[\alpha \beta+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}-\frac{\alpha}{\beta}-\frac{\beta}{\alpha}\right]\left[\alpha \beta+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}+2\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{9}{2}\left[\frac{5}{2}-\frac{\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}}{2}\right]=\frac{9}{4}{/tex}[5 - (p2 - 4)]
= {tex}\frac {9}{4}{/tex}(9 - p2) {tex}\left[\because \alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}=(\alpha+\beta)^{2}-2 \alpha \beta\right]{/tex}

questions.question: The set of all real values of $\lambda$ for which the quadratic equations, ($\lambda^2$ + 1)x^2 - 4$\lambda$x + 2 = 0 always have exactly one root in the interval (0, 1) is
questions.question_raw: The set of all real values of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the quadratic equations, ({tex}\lambda^2{/tex} + 1)x2 - 4{tex}\lambda{/tex}x + 2 = 0 always have exactly one root in the interval (0, 1) is
questions.explanation:
The given quadratic equation is
{tex}\left(\lambda^{2}+1\right) x^{2}-4 \lambda x+2{/tex} = 0
{tex}\because{/tex} One root is in the interval (0, 1)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} f(0)f(1) {tex}\leq{/tex} 0
{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left(\lambda^{2}+1-4 \lambda+2\right) \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left(\lambda^{2}-4 \lambda+3\right) \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}(\lambda-1)(\lambda-3) \leq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lambda \in[1,3]{/tex}
But at {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1 both roots are 1 so {tex}\lambda \neq 1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \lambda \in{/tex} (1, 3)

questions.question: Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the roots of the equation, 5x^2 + 6x - 2 = 0. If S_n = $\alpha^{n}+\beta^{n}$, n = 1, 2, 3,..., then:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of the equation, 5x2 + 6x - 2 = 0. If Sn = {tex}\alpha^{n}+\beta^{n}{/tex}, n = 1, 2, 3,..., then:
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots of the equaton
5x2 + 6x - 2 = 0
Then, {tex}5 \alpha^{2}+6 \alpha-2{/tex} = 0, {tex}5 \beta^{2}+6 \beta-2{/tex} = 0
{tex}5 \alpha^{2}+6 \alpha{/tex} = 2
5S6 + 6S5 = {tex}5\left(\alpha^{6}+\beta^{6}\right)+6\left(\alpha^{5}+\beta^{5}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left(5 \alpha^{4}+6 \alpha^{5}\right)+\left(5 \beta^{6}+6 \beta^{5}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\alpha^{4}\left(5 \alpha^{2}+6 \alpha\right)+\beta^{4}\left(5 \beta^{2}+6 \beta\right){/tex}
= {tex}2\left(\alpha^{4}+\beta^{4}\right){/tex} = 2S4

questions.question: The number of real roots of the equation, e^4x + e^3x - 4e^2x + e^x + 1 = 0 is:
questions.question_raw: The number of real roots of the equation, e4x + e3x - 4e2x + ex + 1 = 0 is:
questions.explanation:
Let ex = {tex}t \in(0, \infty){/tex}
Given equation
t4 + t3 - 4t2 + t + 1 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t2 + t - 4 + {tex}\frac{1}{t}+\frac{1}{t^{2}}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(t^{2}+\frac{1}{t^{2}}\right)+\left(t+\frac{1}{t}\right)-4{/tex} = 0
Let t + {tex}\frac {1}{t}{/tex} = y
(y2 - 2) + y - 4 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y2 + y - 6 = 0
y2 + y - 6 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -3, 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t + {tex}\frac {1}{t}{/tex} = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ex + e-x = 2
x = 0, is the only solution of the equation.
Hence, there only one solution of the given equation.

questions.question: If the equation, x^2 + bx + 45 = 0 (b $\in$ R) has conjugate complex roots and they satisfy |z + 1| = $2 \sqrt{10}$, then:
questions.question_raw: If the equation, x2 + bx + 45 = 0 (b {tex}\in{/tex} R) has conjugate complex roots and they satisfy |z + 1| = {tex}2 \sqrt{10}{/tex}, then:
questions.explanation:
Let z = {tex}\alpha \pm \mathrm{i} \beta{/tex} be the complex roots of the equation
So, sum of roots = 2{tex}\alpha{/tex} = -b and
Product of roots = {tex}\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}{/tex} = 45
{tex}(\alpha+1)^{2}+\beta^{2}{/tex} = 40
Given, {tex}|z+1|=2 \sqrt{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\alpha+1)^{2}-\alpha^{2}{/tex} = -5 {tex}\left[\because \beta^{2}=45-\alpha^{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\alpha{/tex} + 1 = -5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\alpha{/tex} = -6
Hence, b = 6 and b2 - b = 30

questions.question: If $\frac{3+i \sin \theta}{4-i \cos \theta}$, $\theta \in[0,2 \pi]$, is a real number, then an argument of sin$\theta$ + i cos$\theta$ is:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\frac{3+i \sin \theta}{4-i \cos \theta}{/tex}, {tex}\theta \in[0,2 \pi]{/tex}, is a real number, then an argument of sin{tex}\theta{/tex} + i cos{tex}\theta{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Let z = {tex}\frac{3+i \sin \theta}{4-i \cos \theta}{/tex}, after rationalising
z = {tex}\frac{(3+i \sin \theta)}{(4-i \cos \theta)} \times \frac{(4+i \cos \theta)}{(4+i \cos \theta)}{/tex}
As z is purely real
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + 4 sin{tex}\theta{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} tan{tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}- \frac {3}{4}{/tex}
arg(sin{tex}\theta{/tex} + i cos{tex}\theta{/tex}) = {tex}\pi{/tex} + tan-1{tex}\left(\frac{\cos \theta}{\sin \theta}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\pi{/tex} + tan-1{tex}\left(-\frac{4}{3}\right){/tex} = {tex}\pi{/tex} - tan-1{tex}\left(\frac{4}{3}\right){/tex}

questions.question: Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the roots of the equation x^2 - x - 1 = 0. If p_k = $(\alpha)^{k}+(\beta)^{k}$, k $\geq$ 1, then which one of the following statements is not true?
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of the equation x2 - x - 1 = 0. If pk = {tex}(\alpha)^{k}+(\beta)^{k}{/tex}, k {tex}\geq{/tex} 1, then which one of the following statements is not true?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\alpha^{5}=5 \alpha+3{/tex}
{tex}\beta^{5}=5 \beta+3{/tex}
p5 = 5({tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\beta{/tex}) + 6 = 5(1) + 6
[{tex}\because{/tex} from x2 - x - 1 = 0, {tex}\alpha+\beta=\frac{-b}{a}=1{/tex}]
p5 = 11 and p5 = {tex}\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\alpha+1+\beta+1{/tex}
p2 = 3 and p3 = {tex}\alpha^{3}+\beta^{3}{/tex} = {tex}2 \alpha+1+2 \beta+1{/tex}
= 2(1) + 2 = 4
p2 {tex}\times{/tex} p3 = 12 and p5 = 11 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p5 {tex}\neq{/tex} p2 {tex}\times{/tex} p3

questions.question: Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be two real roots ofthe equation (k + 1) tan^2x - $\sqrt{2}$. $\lambda$ tanx = (1 - k), where k($\neq$ -1) and $\lambda$ are real numbers. If tan^2($\alpha$ + $\alpha$) = 50, then a value of $\lambda$ is:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} be two real roots ofthe equation (k + 1) tan2x - {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}. {tex}\lambda{/tex} tanx = (1 - k), where k({tex}\neq{/tex} -1) and {tex}\lambda{/tex} are real numbers. If tan2({tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\alpha{/tex}) = 50, then a value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
(k + 1) tan2x - {tex}\sqrt{2 \lambda}{/tex} tan x + (k - 1) = 0
tan{tex}\alpha{/tex} + tan{tex}\beta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2} \lambda}{k+1}{/tex} [Sum of roots]
{tex}\tan \alpha \cdot \tan \beta=\frac{k-1}{k+1}{/tex} [Product of roots]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} tan{tex}(\alpha+\beta){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{\sqrt{2} \lambda}{k+1}}{1-\frac{k-1}{k+1}}=\frac{\sqrt{2 \lambda}}{2}=\frac{\lambda}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
tan2{tex}(\alpha+\beta){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\lambda^{2}}{2}{/tex} = 50
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 10

questions.question: The value of $\left(\frac{-1+i \sqrt{3}}{1-i}\right)^{30}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}\left(\frac{-1+i \sqrt{3}}{1-i}\right)^{30}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} -1 + {tex}\sqrt{3} i{/tex} = {tex}2 \cdot e^{\frac{2 \pi}{3} i}{/tex} and 1 - i = {tex}\sqrt{2} \cdot e^{-\frac{i \pi}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left(\frac{-1+\sqrt{3} i}{1-i}\right)^{30}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\sqrt{2} e^{\left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) i}\right)^{30}{/tex}
= {tex}2^{15} \cdot e^{-\frac{\pi}{2} i}{/tex} = -215 i

questions.question: Suppose a, b denote the distinct real roots of the quadratic polynomial x^2 + 20x - 2020 and suppose c, d denote the distinct complex roots of the quadratic polynomial x^2 - 20x + 2020. Then the value of ac(a - c) + ad(a - d) + bc(b - c) + bd(b - d) is
questions.question_raw: Suppose a, b denote the distinct real roots of the quadratic polynomial x2 + 20x - 2020 and suppose c, d denote the distinct complex roots of the quadratic polynomial x2 - 20x + 2020. Then the value of ac(a - c) + ad(a - d) + bc(b - c) + bd(b - d) is
questions.explanation:
Consider the quadratic polynomials in the form of equation x2 + 20x - 2020 = 0 ...(i)
x2 - 20x + 2020 = 0 ...(ii)
Since, a and b are roots of the equation (i), then a + b = -20, ab = -2020
{tex}\because{/tex} c and d are the roots of the equation (ii), then c + d = 20, cd = 2020
Now,
ac(a - c) + ad(a - d) + bc(b - c) + bd(b - d)
= a2c - ac2 + a2d - ad2 + b2c - bc2 + b2d - bd2
= a2(c + d) + b2(c + d ) - c2(a + b) - d2(a + b)
= (c + d)(a2 + b2) - (a + b) (c2 + d2)
= (c + d) ((a + b)2 - 2ab) - (a + b) ((c + d)2 - 2cd)
= 20[(20)2 + 4040] + 20[(20)2 - 4040]
= 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 800 = 16000

questions.question: If an angle A of a $\Delta$ABC satisfies 5 cos A + 3 = 0, then the roots of the quadratic equation, 9x^2 + 27x + 20 = 0 are
questions.question_raw: If an angle A of a {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC satisfies 5 cos A + 3 = 0, then the roots of the quadratic equation, 9x2 + 27x + 20 = 0 are
questions.explanation:
Here, 9x2 + 27x + 20 = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4 a c}}{2 a}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{-27 \pm \sqrt{27^{2}-4 \times 9 \times 20}}{2 \times 9}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}-\frac{4}{3},-\frac{5}{3}{/tex}
Given, cos A = {tex}-\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} sec A = {tex}\frac{1}{\cos A}=-\frac{5}{3}{/tex}
Here, A is an obtuse angle.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} tan A = {tex}-\sqrt{\sec ^{2} A-1}=-\frac{4}{3}{/tex}
Hence, roots of the equation are sec A and tan A.

questions.question: If z_1, z_2 are complex numbers such that Re(z_1) = $\left|z_{1}-1\right|$, Re(z_2) = $\left|z_{2}-1\right|$ and arg(z_1 - z_2) = $\frac{\pi}{6}$, then Im(z_1 + z_2) is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If z1, z2 are complex numbers such that Re(z1) = {tex}\left|z_{1}-1\right|{/tex}, Re(z2) = {tex}\left|z_{2}-1\right|{/tex} and arg(z1 - z2) = {tex}\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}, then Im(z1 + z2) is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Let z1 = x1 + iy1 and z2 = x2 + iy2
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|z_{1}-1\right|{/tex} = Re(z1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x1 - 1)2 + {tex}y_{1}^{2}=x_{1}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y_{1}^{2}{/tex} - 2x1 + 1 = 0 ...(i)
{tex}\left|z_{2}-1\right|{/tex} = Re(z2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x2 - 1)2 + {tex}y_{2}^{2}=x_{2}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y_{2}^{2}{/tex} - 2x2 + 1 = 0 ...(ii)
From eqn. (i) - (ii),
{tex}y_{1}^{2}-y_{2}^{2}{/tex} - 2(x1 - x2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y1 + y2 = {tex}2\left(\frac{x_{1}-x_{2}}{y_{1}-y_{2}}\right){/tex} ...(iii)
{tex}\because{/tex} arg(z1 - z2) = {tex}\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} tan-1{tex}\left(\frac{y_{1}-y_{2}}{x_{1}-x_{2}}\right)=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y_{1}-y_{2}}{x_{1}-x_{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2}{y_{1}+y_{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\text { From, } \frac{y_{1}-y_{2}}{x_{1}-x_{2}}=\frac{2}{y_{1}+y_{2}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y1 + y2 = {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1m(z1 + z2) = {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation 375x^2 - 25x - 2 = 0, then $\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_\limits{r=1}^{n} \alpha^{r}+\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_\limits{r=1}^{n} \beta^{r}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots of the equation 375x2 - 25x - 2 = 0, then {tex}\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_\limits{r=1}^{n} \alpha^{r}+\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_\limits{r=1}^{n} \beta^{r}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given equation is, 375x2 - 25x - 2 = 0
Sum and product of the roots are,
{tex}\alpha+\beta=\frac{25}{375}{/tex} and {tex}\alpha \beta=\frac{-2}{375}{/tex}
{tex}\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_\limits{r=1}^{n}\left(\alpha^{r}+\beta^{\prime}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left(\alpha+\alpha^{2}+\alpha^{3}+\ldots \infty\left(\beta+\beta^{2}+\beta^{3} \ldots+\infty\right)\right.{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\alpha}{1-\alpha}+\frac{\beta}{1-\beta}=\frac{\alpha+\beta-2 \alpha \beta}{1-(\alpha+\beta)+\alpha \beta}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac{25}{375}+\frac{4}{375}}{1-\frac{25}{375}-\frac{2}{375}}=\frac{29}{375-25-2}=\frac{29}{348}=\frac{1}{12}{/tex}

questions.question: Let z be a complex number such that $\left|\frac{z-i}{z+2 i}\right|$ = 1 and $|z|=\frac{5}{2}$. Then the value of $|z+3 i|$ is:
questions.question_raw: Let z be a complex number such that {tex}\left|\frac{z-i}{z+2 i}\right|{/tex} = 1 and {tex}|z|=\frac{5}{2}{/tex}. Then the value of {tex}|z+3 i|{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Let z = x + iy
Then, {tex}\left|\frac{z-i}{z+2 i}\right|{/tex} = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + (y - 1)2
= x2 + (y + 2)2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2y + 1 = 4y + 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6y = -3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}- \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\because|z|=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + y2 = {tex}\frac {25}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = {tex}\frac {24}{2}{/tex} = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} z = x + iy {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = {tex}\pm \sqrt{6}-\frac{i}{2}{/tex}
{tex}|z+3 i|=\sqrt{6+\frac{25}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\frac{49}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|z+3 i|{/tex} = {tex}\frac {7}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If tan A and tan B are the roots of the quadratic equation, 3x^2 - 10x - 25 = 0 then the value of 3 sin^2(A + B) - 10 sin (A + B) cos (A + B) - 25 cos^2 (A + B)
questions.question_raw: If tan A and tan B are the roots of the quadratic equation, 3x2 - 10x - 25 = 0 then the value of 3 sin2(A + B) - 10 sin (A + B) cos (A + B) - 25 cos2 (A + B)
questions.explanation:
As tan A and tan B are the roots of 3x2 - 10x - 25 = 0,
So, tan(A + B) = {tex}\frac{\tan A+\tan B}{1-\tan A \tan B}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{10}{3}}{1+\frac{25}{3}}=\frac{10 / 3}{28 / 3}=\frac{5}{14}{/tex}
Now, cos2(A + B)= -1 + 2 cos2(A + B)
= {tex}\frac{1-\tan ^{2}(A+B)}{1+\tan ^{2}(A+B)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cos2(A + B) = {tex}\frac{196}{221}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3sin2 (A + B) - 10 sin (A + B) cos (A + B) - 25 cos2 (A + B)
= cos2 (A + B) [3 tan2(A + B) - 10 tan (A + B) - 25]
= {tex}\frac{75-700-4900}{196} \times \frac{196}{221}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{5525}{196} \times \frac{196}{221}{/tex} = -25

questions.question: The sum of all the real values of x satisfying the equation $2^{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+5 x-50\right)}$ = 1 is:
questions.question_raw: The sum of all the real values of x satisfying the equation {tex}2^{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+5 x-50\right)}{/tex} = 1 is:
questions.explanation:
(x - 1)(x2 + 5x - 50) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1)(x + 10)(x - 5) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, 5, -10
Sum = -4

questions.question: If x is a solution of the equation, $\sqrt{2 x+1}-\sqrt{2 x-1}=1$, $\left(x \geq \frac{1}{2}\right)$, then $\sqrt{4 x^{2}-1}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If x is a solution of the equation, {tex}\sqrt{2 x+1}-\sqrt{2 x-1}=1{/tex}, {tex}\left(x \geq \frac{1}{2}\right){/tex}, then {tex}\sqrt{4 x^{2}-1}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{2 x+1}-\sqrt{2 x-1}{/tex} = 1 ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x + 1 + 2x - 1 - {tex}2 \sqrt{4 x^{2}-1}{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x - 1 = {tex}2 \sqrt{4 x^{2}-1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16x2 - 8x + 1 = 16x2 - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac {5}{8}{/tex} which satisfies equation (i)
So, {tex}\sqrt{4 x^{2}-1}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: The set of all $\alpha$ $\in$ R, for which w = $\frac{1+(1-8 \alpha) z}{1-z}$ is purely imaginary number, for all z $\in$ C satisfying $|z|$ = 1 and Re z $\neq$ 1, is
questions.question_raw: The set of all {tex}\alpha{/tex} {tex}\in{/tex} R, for which w = {tex}\frac{1+(1-8 \alpha) z}{1-z}{/tex} is purely imaginary number, for all z {tex}\in{/tex} C satisfying {tex}|z|{/tex} = 1 and Re z {tex}\neq{/tex} 1, is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because|z|{/tex} = 1 & Re z {tex}\neq{/tex} 1
Suppose z = x + iy {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + y2 = 1 ...(i)
Now, w = {tex}\frac{1+(1-8 \alpha) z}{1-z}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} w = {tex}\frac{1+(1-8 \alpha)(x+i y)}{1-(x+i y)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} w = {tex}\frac{1+(1-8 \alpha)(x+i y))((1-x)+i y)}{1-(x+i y))((1-x)+i y)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} w = {tex}\frac{\left[\left(1+x(1-8 \alpha)(1-x)-(1-8 \alpha) y^{2}\right]\right.}{(1-x)^{2}+y^{2}}{/tex} + {tex}i \frac{[(1+x(1-8 \alpha)) y-(1-8 \alpha) y(1-x)]}{(1-x)^{2}+y^{2}}{/tex}
As, w is purely imaginary. So,
Re w = {tex}\frac{\left[(1+x(1-8 \alpha))(1-x)-(1-8 \alpha) y^{2}\right]}{(1-x)^{2}+y^{2}}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 - x) + x(1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex}) (1 - x) = (1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex})y2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 - x) + x (1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex}) - x2(1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex}) = (1 - 8x)}y2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 - x) + x (1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex}) = 1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex} [From (i), x2 + y2 = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 - 8{tex}\alpha{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 0
{tex}\therefore \alpha \in\{0\}{/tex}

questions.question: The sum of the roots of the equation, x^2 + |2x - 3| - 4 = 0, is:
questions.question_raw: The sum of the roots of the equation, x2 + |2x - 3| - 4 = 0, is:
questions.explanation:
x2 + |2x - 3| - 4 = 0
|2x - 3| = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{l} (2 x-3) \text { if } \quad x>\frac{3}{2} \\ -(2 x-3) \text { if } x<\frac{3}{2} \end{array}\right.{/tex}
for x > {tex}\frac {3}{2}{/tex}, x2 + 2x - 3 - 4 = 0
x2 + 2x - 7 = 0
x = {tex}\frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{4+28}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-2 \pm 4 \sqrt{2}}{2}=-1 \pm 2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
Here x = {tex}2 \sqrt{2}-1{/tex} {tex}\left\{2 \sqrt{2}-1<\frac{3}{2}\right\}{/tex}
for x < {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
x2 - 2x +  3 - 4 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 - 2x - 1 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{2 \pm \sqrt{4+4}}{2}=\frac{2 \pm 2 \sqrt{2}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}1 \pm \sqrt{2}{/tex}
Here x = 1 - {tex}\sqrt 2{/tex} {tex}\left\{(1-\sqrt{2})<\frac{3}{2}\right\}{/tex}
Sum of roots: {tex}(2 \sqrt{2}-1)+(1-\sqrt{2}){/tex} = {tex}\sqrt 2{/tex}

questions.question: If z is a non-real complex number, then the minimum value of $\frac{Im z^{5}}{(Im z)^{5}}$ is:
questions.question_raw: If z is a non-real complex number, then the minimum value of {tex}\frac{Im z^{5}}{(Im z)^{5}}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Let z = {tex}\mathrm{re}^{\mathrm{i} \theta}{/tex}
Consider {tex}\frac{\operatorname{Imz}^{5}}{(\operatorname{Imz})^{5}}=\frac{r^{5}(\sin 5 \theta)}{r^{5}(\sin \theta)^{5}}{/tex}
({tex}\because{/tex} {tex}e^{i \theta}{/tex} = cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex})
= {tex}\frac{\sin 5 \theta}{\sin ^{5} \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16 \sin ^{5} \theta-20 \sin ^{3} \theta+5 \sin \theta}{\sin ^{5} \theta}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{16 \sin ^{5} \theta}{\sin ^{5} \theta}-\frac{20 \sin ^{3} \theta}{\sin ^{5} \theta}+\frac{5 \sin \theta}{\sin ^{5} \theta}{/tex}
= 5 cosec4{tex}\theta{/tex} - 20 cosec2{tex}\theta{/tex} + 16
minimum value of {tex}\frac{\operatorname{Im} z^{5}}{(\mathrm{Imz})^{5}}{/tex} is -4

questions.question: If p and q are non-zero real numbers and $\alpha^{3}+\beta^{3}$ = -p, $\alpha \beta$ = q, then a quadratic equation whose roots are $\frac{\alpha^{2}}{\beta}, \frac{\beta^{2}}{\alpha}$ is:
questions.question_raw: If p and q are non-zero real numbers and {tex}\alpha^{3}+\beta^{3}{/tex} = -p, {tex}\alpha \beta{/tex} = q, then a quadratic equation whose roots are {tex}\frac{\alpha^{2}}{\beta}, \frac{\beta^{2}}{\alpha}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\alpha^{3}+\beta^{3}{/tex} = -p and {tex}\alpha \beta{/tex} = q
Let {tex}\frac{\alpha^{2}}{\beta}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\beta^{2}}{\alpha}{/tex} be the root of required quadratic equation.
So, {tex}\frac{\alpha^{2}}{\beta}+\frac{\beta^{2}}{\alpha}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\alpha^{3}+\beta^{3}}{\alpha \beta}=\frac{-p}{q}{/tex}
and {tex}\frac{\alpha^{2}}{\beta} \times \frac{\beta^{2}}{\alpha}{/tex} = {tex}\alpha \beta{/tex} = q
Hence, required quadratic equation is
{tex}x^{2}-\left(\frac{-p}{q}\right){/tex}x + q = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}x + q = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} qx2 + px + q2 = 0

questions.question: If $\frac{w-\bar{w} z}{1-z}$ is purely real where w = $\alpha$ + i$\beta$, $\beta \neq 0$ and z $\neq$ 1, then the set of the values of z is
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\frac{w-\bar{w} z}{1-z}{/tex} is purely real where w = {tex}\alpha{/tex} + i{tex}\beta{/tex}, {tex}\beta \neq 0{/tex} and z {tex}\neq{/tex} 1, then the set of the values of z is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{w-w z}{1-z}{/tex} is purely real
{tex}\therefore\left(\frac{w-\bar{w} z}{1-z}\right)=\left(\frac{w-\bar{w} z}{1-z}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\bar{w}-w \bar{z}}{1-\bar{z}}=\frac{w-\bar{w} z}{1-z}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \bar{w}-\bar{w} z-w \bar{z}+w z \bar{z}{/tex} = {tex}w-w \bar{z}-\bar{w} z+\bar{w} z \bar{z}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow w-\bar{w}=(w-\bar{w})|z|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|z|^{2}{/tex} = 1 ({tex}\because{/tex} w = {tex}\alpha+i \beta{/tex} and {tex}\beta \neq 0{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow|z|{/tex} = 1 and also given that z {tex}\neq{/tex} 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The required set is {z : {tex}|z|{/tex} = 1, z {tex}\neq{/tex} 1} = {tex}3 \omega(\omega-1){/tex}

questions.question: Let (x_0, y_0) be the solution of the following equations $(2 x)^{\ell n2}=(3 y)^{\ell n3} ; 3^{\ell nx}=2^{\ell ny} .$ Then x_0 is
questions.question_raw: Let (x0, y0) be the solution of the following equations {tex}(2 x)^{\ell n2}=(3 y)^{\ell n3} ; 3^{\ell nx}=2^{\ell ny} .{/tex} Then x0 is
questions.explanation:
Given : (2x){tex}\ell{/tex}n2 = (3y){tex}\ell{/tex}n3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}n2{tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = {tex}\ell{/tex}n3{tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}n3y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}n2{tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = {tex}\ell{/tex}n3{tex}\cdot{/tex}({tex}\ell{/tex}n3 + {tex}\ell{/tex}ny) ...(i)
Also given : 3{tex}\ell{/tex}nx = 2{tex}\ell{/tex}ny
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}nx. {tex}\ell{/tex}n3 = {tex}\ell{/tex}ny. {tex}\ell{/tex}n2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}ny = {tex}\frac{\ell n x \cdot \ell n 3}{\ell n 2}{/tex} ...(ii)
From equation (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}\ell{/tex}n2. {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = {tex}\ell{/tex}n3 {tex}\left[ ​​\ell n 3+\frac{\ell n x \cdot \ell n 3}{\ell n 2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ({tex}\ell{/tex}n2)2 {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = ({tex}\ell{/tex}n3)2 {tex}\ell{/tex}n2 + ({tex}\ell{/tex}n3)2 {tex}\ell{/tex}nx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ({tex}\ell{/tex}n2)2 {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = ({tex}\ell{/tex}n3)2 ({tex}\ell{/tex}n2 + {tex}\ell{/tex}nx)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ({tex}\ell{/tex}n2)2 {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x - ({tex}\ell{/tex}n3)2 {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [({tex}\ell{/tex}n2)2 - ({tex}\ell{/tex}n3)2] {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\ell{/tex}n2x = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Let z = x + iy be a non-zero complex number such that z^2 = i$|z|^{2}$, where i = $\sqrt{-1}$ , then z lies on the:
questions.question_raw: Let z = x + iy be a non-zero complex number such that z2 = i{tex}|z|^{2}{/tex}, where i = {tex}\sqrt{-1}{/tex} , then z lies on the:
questions.explanation:
Let z = x + iy
{tex}\because{/tex} z2 = i{tex}|z|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x2 - y2 + 2ixy = i(x2 + y2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 - y2 = 0 and 2xy = x2 + y2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y) = 0 and (x - y)2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = y

questions.question: Let p, q $\in$ {1, 2, 3, 4}. The number of equations of the form px^2 + qx + 1 = 0 having real roots is
questions.question_raw: Let p, q {tex}\in{/tex} {1, 2, 3, 4}. The number of equations of the form px2 + qx + 1 = 0 having real roots is
questions.explanation:
For the equation px2 + qx + 1 = 0 to have real roots
D {tex}\geq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} q2 {tex}\geq{/tex} 4p
If p = 1 then q2 {tex}\geq{/tex} 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} q = 2, 3, 4
If p = 2 then q2 {tex}\geq{/tex} 8 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} q = 3, 4
If p = 3 then q2 {tex}\geq{/tex} 12 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} q = 4
If p = 4 then q2 {tex}\geq{/tex} 16 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} q = 4
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Number of required equations = 7

questions.question: If a and b are real numbers such that (2 + $\alpha$)^4 = a + b$\alpha$. where $\alpha$ = $\frac{-1+i \sqrt{3}}{2}$, then a + b is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If a and b are real numbers such that (2 + {tex}\alpha{/tex})4 = a + b{tex}\alpha{/tex}. where {tex}\alpha{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1+i \sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}, then a + b is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given that, {tex}\alpha=\frac{-1+\sqrt{3} i}{2}=\omega{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (2 + {tex}\omega{/tex})4 = a + b{tex}\omega{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4 + {tex}\omega^2{/tex} + 4{tex}\omega{/tex})2 = a + b{tex}\omega{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\omega^{2}+4(1+\omega)\right)^{2}{/tex} = a + b{tex}\omega{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\omega^{2}-4 \omega^{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} = a + b{tex}\omega{/tex}
[{tex}\because{/tex} 1 + {tex}\omega{/tex} = -{tex}\omega^2{/tex}] ({tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\omega^3{/tex} = 1)
On comparing, a = 0, b = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b = 0 + 9 = 9

questions.question: If (x^2 + px + 1) is a factor of (ax^3 + bx + c), then
questions.question_raw: If (x2 + px + 1) is a factor of (ax3 + bx + c), then
questions.explanation:
Since, (x2 + px + 1) is a factor of ax3 + bx + c, hence we can assume that zeros of x2 + px + 1 are {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} and that of ax3 + bx + c be {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex}, {tex}\gamma{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \alpha+\beta{/tex} = -p ...(i)
{tex}\alpha \beta{/tex} = 1 ...(ii)
and {tex}\alpha+\beta+\gamma=0{/tex} ...(iii)
{tex}\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha=\frac{\mathrm{b}}{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} ...(iv)
{tex}\alpha \beta \gamma=\frac{-c}{a}{/tex} ...(v)
On solving (ii) and (v), we get {tex}\gamma{/tex} = {tex}-\frac {c}{a}{/tex}
On solving (i) and (iii), we get {tex}\gamma{/tex} = p
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p = {tex}\gamma{/tex} = {tex}-\frac {c}{a}{/tex}
Using equations (i), (ii) and (iv), we get
{tex}1+\gamma(-p)=\frac{b}{a}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 1+\left(-\frac{c}{a}\right)\left(\frac{c}{a}\right)=\frac{b}{a}{/tex} {tex}(\because \gamma = p = - \frac {c}{a}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 1-\frac{c^{2}}{a^{2}}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 - c2 = ab

questions.question: The value of $\left(\frac{1+\sin \frac{2 \pi}{9}+i \cos \frac{2 \pi}{9}}{1+\sin \frac{2 \pi}{9}-i \cos \frac{2 \pi}{9}}\right)^{3}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}\left(\frac{1+\sin \frac{2 \pi}{9}+i \cos \frac{2 \pi}{9}}{1+\sin \frac{2 \pi}{9}-i \cos \frac{2 \pi}{9}}\right)^{3}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{1+\cos \frac{5 \pi}{18}+i \sin \frac{5 \pi}{18}}{1+\cos \frac{5 \pi}{18}-i \sin \frac{5 \pi}{18}}\right)^{3}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{2 \cos ^{2} \frac{5 \pi}{36}+i 2 \sin \frac{5 \pi}{36} \cdot \cos \frac{5 \pi}{36}}{2 \cos ^{2} \frac{5 \pi}{36}-i 2 \sin \frac{5 \pi}{36} \cdot \cos \frac{5 \pi}{36}}\right)^{3}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{\cos \frac{5 \pi}{36}+i \sin \frac{5 \pi}{36}}{\cos \frac{5 \pi}{36}-i \sin \frac{5 \pi}{36}}\right)^{3}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\cos \frac{5 \pi}{36}+i \sin \frac{5 \pi}{36}\right)^{6}{/tex}
= {tex}\cos \left(6 \times \frac{5 \pi}{36}\right){/tex} + i sin{tex}\left(6 \times \frac{5 \pi}{36}\right){/tex} = cos{tex}\frac{5 \pi}{6}{/tex} + i sin{tex}\frac{5 \pi}{6}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+i \frac{1}{2}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}(\sqrt{3}-i){/tex}

questions.question: The imaginary part of $(3+2 \sqrt{-54})^{1 / 2}-(3-2 \sqrt{-54})^{1 / 2}$ can be:
questions.question_raw: The imaginary part of {tex}(3+2 \sqrt{-54})^{1 / 2}-(3-2 \sqrt{-54})^{1 / 2}{/tex} can be:
questions.explanation:
3 + {tex}2 \sqrt{-54}{/tex} = 3 + {tex}6 \sqrt{6} i{/tex}
Let {tex}\sqrt{3+6 \sqrt{6 i}}{/tex} = a + ib
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 - b2 = 3 and ab = {tex}3 \sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + b2 = {tex}\sqrt{\left(a^{2}-b^{2}\right)^{2}+4 a^{2} b^{2}}{/tex} = 15
So, a = {tex}\pm 3{/tex} and b = {tex}\pm \sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt{3+6 \sqrt{6} i}{/tex} = {tex}\pm(3+\sqrt{6} i){/tex}
Similarly, {tex}\sqrt{3+6 \sqrt{6} i}{/tex} = {tex}\pm(3+\sqrt{6} i){/tex}
lm{tex}(\sqrt{3+6 \sqrt{6} i}-\sqrt{3-6 \sqrt{6} i}){/tex} = {tex}\pm 2 \sqrt{6}{/tex}

questions.question: Let $\alpha=\frac{-1+i \sqrt{3}}{2}$. If a = (1 + $\alpha$)$\sum \limits_{k=0}^{100} \alpha^{2 k}$ and b = $\sum \limits_{k=0}^{100} \alpha^{3 k}$, then a and b are the roots of the quadratic equation:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}\alpha=\frac{-1+i \sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}. If a = (1 + {tex}\alpha{/tex}){tex}\sum \limits_{k=0}^{100} \alpha^{2 k}{/tex} and b = {tex}\sum \limits_{k=0}^{100} \alpha^{3 k}{/tex}, then a and b are the roots of the quadratic equation:
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\alpha=\omega{/tex}, b = 1 + {tex}\omega^{3}{/tex} + {tex}\omega^{6}{/tex} + ... = 101
a = (1 + {tex}\omega{/tex}) (1 + {tex}\omega^2{/tex} + {tex}\omega^4{/tex} + ... {tex}\omega^{198}{/tex} + {tex}\omega^{200}{/tex})
= {tex}(1+\omega) \frac{\left(1-\left(\omega^{2}\right)^{101}\right)}{1-\omega^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(\omega+1)\left(\omega^{202}-1\right)}{\left(\omega^{2}-1\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = {tex}\frac{(1+\omega)(1-\omega)}{1-m^{2}}{/tex} = 1
Required equation = x2 - (101 + 1)x + (101) {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 - 102x + 101 = 0

questions.question:
Let p, q be integers and let $\alpha$, $\beta$ be the roots of the equation, x^2 - x - 1 = 0, where $\alpha$ $\neq$ $\beta$. For n = 0, 1, 2, ...., let a_n = $\mathrm{p} \alpha^{\mathrm{n}}+\mathrm{q} \beta^{\mathrm{n}}$.
FACT: If a and b are rational numbers and a + $b \sqrt 5$ = 0, then a = 0 = b
a_12 = ________
questions.question_raw:
Let p, q be integers and let {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of the equation, x2 - x - 1 = 0, where {tex}\alpha{/tex} {tex}\neq{/tex} {tex}\beta{/tex}. For n = 0, 1, 2, ...., let an = {tex}\mathrm{p} \alpha^{\mathrm{n}}+\mathrm{q} \beta^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex}.
FACT: If a and b are rational numbers and a + {tex}b \sqrt 5{/tex} = 0, then a = 0 = b
a12 = ________
questions.explanation:
{tex}\alpha^{2}=\alpha+1{/tex}
{tex}\beta^{2}=\beta+1{/tex}
an = {tex}p \alpha^{n}+q \beta^{n}{/tex}
= {tex}p\left(\alpha^{n-1}+\alpha^{n-2}\right){/tex} + {tex}q\left(\beta^{n-1}+\beta^{n-2}\right){/tex}
= an-1 + an-2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a12 = a11 + a10

questions.question: If Re$\left(\frac{z-1}{2 z+i}\right)$ = 1, where z = x + iy, then the point (x, y) lies on a:
questions.question_raw: If Re{tex}\left(\frac{z-1}{2 z+i}\right){/tex} = 1, where z = x + iy, then the point (x, y) lies on a:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} z = x + iy
{tex}\left(\frac{z-1}{2 z+i}\right)=\frac{(x-1)+i y}{2(x+i y)+i}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(x-1)+i y}{2 x+(2 y+1) i} \times \frac{2 x-(2 y+1) i}{2 x-(2 y+1) i}{/tex}
Re{tex}\left(\frac{z+1}{2 z+i}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x(x-1)+y(2 y+1)}{(2 x)^{2}+(2 y+1)^{2}}{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(y+\frac{3}{4}\right)^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{4}\right)^{2}{/tex}

questions.question:
Let p, q be integers and let $\alpha$, $\beta$ be the roots of the equation, x^2 - x - 1 = 0, where $\alpha$ $\neq$ $\beta$. For n = 0, 1, 2,...., let a_n = $\mathrm{p} \alpha^{\mathrm{n}}+\mathrm{q} \beta^{\mathrm{n}}$.
FACT: If a and b are rational numbers and a + $b \sqrt 5$ = 0, then a = 0 = b
If a_4 = 28, then p + 2q = ________
questions.question_raw:
Let p, q be integers and let {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of the equation, x2 - x - 1 = 0, where {tex}\alpha{/tex} {tex}\neq{/tex} {tex}\beta{/tex}. For n = 0, 1, 2,...., let an = {tex}\mathrm{p} \alpha^{\mathrm{n}}+\mathrm{q} \beta^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex}.
FACT: If a and b are rational numbers and a + {tex}b \sqrt 5{/tex} = 0, then a = 0 = b
If a4 = 28, then p + 2q = ________
questions.explanation:
{tex}\alpha=\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}{/tex}, {tex}\beta=\frac{1-\sqrt{5}}{2}{/tex}
a4 = a3 + a2
= 2a2 + a1
= 3a1 + 2a0
28 = p(3{tex}\alpha{/tex} + 2) + g(3{tex}\beta{/tex} + 2)
28 = (p + q){tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}+2\right){/tex} + (p - q){tex}\left(\frac{3 \sqrt{5}}{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p - q = 0 and (p + q ) {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac {7}{2}{/tex} = 28
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p + q = 8 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = q = 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p + 2q = 12

questions.question: If the four complex numbers z, $\bar{z}$, $\bar{z}$ - 2 Re($\bar{z}$) and z - 2 Re(z) represent the vertices of a square of side 4 units in the Argand plane, then $|z|$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the four complex numbers z, {tex}\bar{z}{/tex}, {tex}\bar{z}{/tex} - 2 Re({tex}\bar{z}{/tex}) and z - 2 Re(z) represent the vertices of a square of side 4 units in the Argand plane, then {tex}|z|{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Let z = x + iy
{tex}\because{/tex} Length of side of square = 4 units
Then, {tex}|z-\bar{z}|{/tex} = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow|2 x|{/tex} = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|x|{/tex} = 2
Also, {tex}|z-(z-2 \operatorname{Re}(z))|{/tex} = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\mid 2 \operatorname{Re}(z)\mid{/tex} = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|2 x|{/tex} = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}| x|{/tex} = 2
{tex}\therefore|z|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}=\sqrt{4+4}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If z be a complex number satisfying |Re(z)| + |Im(z)| = 4, then |z| cannot be:
questions.question_raw: If z be a complex number satisfying |Re(z)| + |Im(z)| = 4, then |z| cannot be:
questions.explanation:
z = x + iy
{tex}|x|+|y|{/tex} = 4
{tex}|z|=\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}{/tex}
Minimum value of {tex}|z|=2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
Maximum value of {tex}|z|{/tex} = 4
{tex}|z| \in[\sqrt{8}, \sqrt{16}]{/tex}
So, {tex}| z |{/tex} can't be {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex}

questions.question: If |z - 3 + 2i| $\leq$ 4 then the difference between the greatest value and the least value of |z| is
questions.question_raw: If |z - 3 + 2i| {tex}\leq{/tex} 4 then the difference between the greatest value and the least value of |z| is
questions.explanation:
{tex}|z-(3-2 i)| \leq 4{/tex} represents a circle whose centre is (3, -2) and radius = 4.
{tex}|z|=|z-0|{/tex} represents the distance of point z from origin (0, 0)

Suppose RS is the normal of the circle passing through origin ‘O’ and G is its center (3, -2).
Here, OR is the least distance
and OS is the greatest distance
OR = RG - OG and OS = OG + GS ...(i)
As, RG = GS = 4
OG = {tex}\sqrt{3^{2}+(-2)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{9+4}=\sqrt{13}{/tex}
From (i), OR = 4 - {tex}\sqrt{13}{/tex} and OS = 4 + {tex}\sqrt{13}{/tex}
So, required difference = (4 + {tex}\sqrt 13{/tex}) - (4 - {tex}\sqrt 13{/tex})
= {tex}\sqrt{13}+\sqrt{13}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{13}{/tex}

questions.question: If the equations x^2 + bx - 1 = 0 and x^2 + x + b = 0 have a common root different from -1, then |b| is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the equations x2 + bx - 1 = 0 and x2 + x + b = 0 have a common root different from -1, then |b| is equal to:
questions.explanation:
x2 + bx - 1 = 0 common root

Put x = {tex}\frac{b+1}{b-1}{/tex} in equation
{tex}\left(\frac{b+1}{b-1}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{b+1}{b-1}\right)+b{/tex} = 0
(b + 1)2 + (b + 1) (b - 1) + b(b - 1)2 = 0
b2 + 1 + 2b + b2 - 1 + b(b2 - 2b + 1) = 0
2b2 + 2b + b3 - 2b2 + b = 0
b3 + 3b = 0
b(b2 + 3) = 0
b2 = -3
b = {tex}\pm \sqrt{3} i{/tex}
|b| = {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: If one root of the equation x^2 + (1 - 3i) x - 2 (1 + i) = 0 is -1 + i, then the other root is
questions.question_raw: If one root of the equation x2 + (1 - 3i) x - 2 (1 + i) = 0 is -1 + i, then the other root is
questions.explanation:
Let the other root be {tex}\beta{/tex}. Then,
(-1 + i) + {tex}\beta{/tex} = sum of roots = -(1 - 3i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \beta{/tex} = -1 + 3i + 1 - i = 2i

questions.question: If 2 + $i \sqrt{3}$ is a root of the equation x^2 + px + q = 0, where p and q are real, then (p, q) =
questions.question_raw: If 2 + {tex}i \sqrt{3}{/tex} is a root of the equation x2 + px + q = 0, where p and q are real, then (p, q) =
questions.explanation:
2 + {tex}i \sqrt{3}{/tex} is one root of the given equation, then 2 - {tex}i \sqrt{3}{/tex} is the other root
Sum of the roots = 4 = -p and product of roots = 7 = q
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (p, q) = (-4, 7)

questions.question: sin^-1 $\left(\frac{z-1}{i}\right)$; where z is non-real, can be an angle of a triangle; if
questions.question_raw: sin-1 {tex}\left(\frac{z-1}{i}\right){/tex}; where z is non-real, can be an angle of a triangle; if
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{z-1}{i}{/tex} must be real and -1 {tex}\leq \frac{z-1}{i} \leq{/tex} 1
{tex}\frac{z-1}{i}=\frac{x-1+i y}{i}{/tex} = y - (x - 1) i is real
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x - 1 = 0 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x = 1 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} Re(z) = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin-1 {tex}\left(\frac{z-1}{i}\right){/tex} = sin-1 y, exists for -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} y {tex}\leq{/tex} 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} Im(z) {tex}\leq{/tex} 1

questions.question: If u + iv = $\frac{3 i}{x+i y+2}$, then y in terms of u, v is
questions.question_raw: If u + iv = {tex}\frac{3 i}{x+i y+2}{/tex}, then y in terms of u, v is
questions.explanation:
x + 2 + iy = {tex}\frac{3 i}{u+i v} \times \frac{(u-i v)}{u-i v}{/tex}
x + 2 + iy = {tex}\frac{3 u i+3 v}{u^{2}+v^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac{3 u}{u^{2}+v^{2}}{/tex}

questions.question: If $x^{\frac{2}{3}}-7 x^{\frac{1}{3}}$ + 10 = 0, then x $\in$
questions.question_raw: If {tex}x^{\frac{2}{3}}-7 x^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} + 10 = 0, then x {tex}\in{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^{\frac{2}{3}}-7 x^{\frac{1}{3}} + 10 = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{2}-7\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}\right) + 10 = 0{/tex}
Leet a = {tex}x^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}, then a2 - 7a + 10 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - 5)(a - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 5, 2
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} = 5, 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 125, 8

questions.question: $\sqrt{-8-6 i}$ =
questions.question_raw: {tex}\sqrt{-8-6 i}{/tex} =

Let z = - 8 - 6i
Here, a = -8, b = -6 and b < 0

questions.question: If the equation x^2 + $\alpha$x + $\beta$ = 0 has equal roots and one of the roots of the equation x^2 + $\alpha$x + 18 = 0 is 3, then the value of $\beta$ is:
questions.question_raw: If the equation x2 + {tex}\alpha{/tex}x + {tex}\beta{/tex} = 0 has equal roots and one of the roots of the equation x2 + {tex}\alpha{/tex}x + 18 = 0 is 3, then the value of {tex}\beta{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
3 is a root of equation x2 + {tex}\alpha{/tex}x + 18 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (3)2 + 3{tex}\alpha{/tex} + 18 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\alpha{/tex} = - 9
For equal roots, discriminant of the equation
x2 + {tex}\alpha{/tex}x + {tex}\beta{/tex} = 0 is 0.
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 81 - 4{tex}\beta{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\beta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{81}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: If x = -5 + 4i and N = x^4 + 9x^3 + 35x^2 - x + 4. Then the sum of the digits of |N| is
questions.question_raw: If x = -5 + 4i and N = x4 + 9x3 + 35x2 - x + 4. Then the sum of the digits of |N| is
questions.explanation:
x = - 5 + 4i
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 5)2 = -16 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x2 + 10x + 41 = 0 ...(i)
N = x4 + 9x3 + 35x2 - x + 4
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} N = x2 (x2 + 10x + 41) - x3 - 6x2 - x + 4
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} N = x2(0) - x (x2 + 10x + 41) + 4x2 + 40x + 4
N = 0 - x(0) + 4(x2 + 10x + 41) - 160 = -160
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |N| = 160
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Sum of the digits of |N| = 1 + 6 + 0 = 7

questions.question: If the roots of the quadratic equation 3x^2 - 6x - log_5 p = 0 are real, then the least value of p is:
questions.question_raw: If the roots of the quadratic equation 3x2 - 6x - log5 p = 0 are real, then the least value of p is:
questions.explanation:
Equation 3x2 - 6x -  log5 p = 0 has real roots
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Discriminant {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 36 + 12 log5 p {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 3 + log5 p {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} log5 p {tex}\ge{/tex} - 3
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} p {tex}\ge{/tex} 5-3
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} p {tex}\ge{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{125}{/tex}
The least value of p = {tex}\frac{1}{125}{/tex}

questions.question: If x^2 + x + 1 is a factor of 7x^3 + Ax + B, then
questions.question_raw: If x2 + x + 1 is a factor of 7x3 + Ax + B, then
questions.explanation:
7x3 + Ax + B = 7 (x3 - 1) + Ax + (B + 7)
If x2 + x + 1 is a factor of 7x3 + Ax + B, then Ax + B + 7 = 0 for all x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 0, B = -7

questions.question: m and n are roots of (x + p) (x + q) - k = 0 then the sum of the roots of the equation (x - m) (x - n) + k = 0 is:
questions.question_raw: m and n are roots of (x + p) (x + q) - k = 0 then the sum of the roots of the equation (x - m) (x - n) + k = 0 is:
questions.explanation:
m and n are roots of the equation
(x + p) (x + q) - k = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + p) (x + q) - k = (x - m) (x - n) for all x
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (x - m) (x - n) + k = (x + p) (x + q) for all x
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (x - m) (x - n) + k = (x - (-p)) (x - (-q)) for all x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The roots of the equation (x - m) (x - n) + k = 0 are -p, -q

questions.question: z_1, z_2, z_3 are complex numbers. Then the expression z_1 Im ($\bar z_{2}$ z_3) + z_2 Im ($\bar z_{3}$z_1) + z_3 Im ($\bar z_{1}$z_2) =

Similarly
z2 Im ({tex}\bar z_{3}{/tex}z1) = {tex}\frac{1}{2i}{/tex} (z1z2{tex}\bar z_{3}{/tex} - z2z3{tex}\bar z_{1}{/tex}) ...(ii)
and z3 Im ({tex}\bar z_{1}{/tex}z2) = {tex}\frac{1}{2i}{/tex} (z2z3{tex}\bar z_{1}{/tex} - z3z1{tex}\bar z_{2}{/tex}) ...(iii)
Adding (i), (ii) and (iii) we get,
Required expression = 0

questions.question: How many integers m are there for which the equation (x^2 + x + 2)^2 - (m - 3)(x^2 + x + 2) (x^2 + x + 1) + (m - 4) (x^2 + x + 2)^2 = 0 has at least one real root?
questions.question_raw: How many integers m are there for which the equation (x2 + x + 2)2 - (m - 3)(x2 + x + 2) (x2 + x + 1) + (m - 4) (x2 + x + 2)2 = 0 has at least one real root?
questions.explanation:
Let x2 + x + 2 = a, x2 + x + 1 = b , then
a - (m - 3)ab - (m - 4) b2 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (a - b) [a - (m - 4)b] = 0 ,a {tex}\neq{/tex} b
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = (m - 4) b
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (5 - m)x2 + (5 - m)x + (6 - m) = 0, m {tex}\neq{/tex} 5
For real roots, (5 - m)2 - 4(5 - m){6 - m) {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (5 - m) (3m - 19) {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (m - 5) (3m - 19) {tex}\le{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 5 {tex}\le{/tex} m {tex}\le{/tex} {tex}\frac{19}{3}{/tex}, m {tex}\neq{/tex} 5
Integer m = 6

questions.question: Consider the equation (1 + 2i) z^3 - 2(3 + i) z^2 + (5 - 4i) z +2a^2 = 0 (a : a real number). The equation has a real root z. Then which of the following is not correct?
questions.question_raw: Consider the equation (1 + 2i) z3 - 2(3 + i) z2 + (5 - 4i) z +2a2 = 0 (a : a real number). The equation has a real root z. Then which of the following is not correct?

options.text: a takes exactly two positive values
a takes exactly one integral value

questions.explanation:
Given z is a real and (1 + 2i) z3 - 2(3 + i) z2 + (5 - 4i)z + 2a2 = 0
= 0 + 0i. Equating real and imaginary parts of L.H.S. to respective parts of R.H.S.
z3 - 6z2 + 5z + 2a2 = 0 ...(i)
2z3 - 2z2 - 4z = 0 ...(ii)
(ii) {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2z (z2 - z - 2) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 0, -1, 2
(i) and z = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 0
(i) and z = -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 1 - 6 - 5 + 2a2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow a=\pm \sqrt{6}{/tex}
(i) and z = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 - 24 + 10 + 2a2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = {tex}\pm \sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Real numbers x, y, z satisfy x + y + z = 4 and x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = 6. If the minimum and maximum values of x are m and M then M - m is:
questions.question_raw: Real numbers x, y, z satisfy x + y + z = 4 and x2 + y2 + z2 = 6. If the minimum and maximum values of x are m and M then M - m is:
questions.explanation:
x2 + y2 + (4 - x - y)2 = 6
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2x2 + 2y2 - 8x - 8y + 2xy + 10 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} y2 + (x - 4)y + (x2 - 4x + 5) = 0
For real y, (x - 4)2 - 4(x2 - 4x + 5) {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} -3x2 - 8x - 4 {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} -3x2 - 8x + 4 {tex}\le{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (3x - 2) (x - 2) {tex}\le{/tex} 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} {tex}\le{/tex} x {tex}\le{/tex} 2
M - m = 2 - {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of the locus of z formed by the equation |z - 1|^2 + |z + 1|^2 = 4 is
questions.question_raw: The area of the locus of z formed by the equation |z - 1|2 + |z + 1|2 = 4 is
questions.explanation:
(x - 1)2 + y2 + (x + 1)2 + y2 = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x2 + 2y2 + 2 = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + y2 = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of locus = {tex}\pi{/tex} sq. units.

questions.question: If P(x) = ax^2 + bx + c, Q(x) = -ax^2 + dx + c where ac $\neq$ 0, then the equation P(x) Q(x) = 0 has:
questions.question_raw: If P(x) = ax2 + bx + c, Q(x) = -ax2 + dx + c where ac {tex}\neq{/tex} 0, then the equation P(x) Q(x) = 0 has:
questions.explanation:
ac {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ac < 0 or ac > 0
If ac < 0 then b2 - 4ac > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(x) = 0 has two real and distinct roots.
If ac > 0 then d2 + 4ac > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Q(x) = 0 has two real and distinct roots.
P(x) Q(x) = 0 has four roots, two of them are roots of P(x) = 0 and other two are of Q(x) = 0.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(x) Q(x) = 0 surely cannot have all non-real roots.i.e., P(x) Q(x) = 0 has at least two real roots.

questions.question: The number of complex numbers z which satisfy z^2 + 2 |z|^2 = 2 is
questions.question_raw: The number of complex numbers z which satisfy z2 + 2 |z|2 = 2 is
questions.explanation:
z2 + 2 |z|2 = 2
z2 = 2 (1 - |z|2) is real ...[R.H.S. is real]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} z is real or purely imaginary
If z is real then z2 = 2(1 - z2)
3z2 = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = {tex}\pm \frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
If z is purely imaginary then z2 = 2(1 + z2)
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} z2 = -2
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} z = {tex}\pm i \sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 complex numbers satisfy the equation.

questions.question: The graphs of y = (a - 6)x^2 - 2 and y = 2ax + 1 do not intersect, then a belongs to:
questions.question_raw: The graphs of y = (a - 6)x2 - 2 and y = 2ax + 1 do not intersect, then a belongs to:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2ax + 1 ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = (a − 6)x2 − 2 ...(ii)
Substituting values of y from (i) into (ii),
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - 6)x2 - 2ax - 3 = 0 (quadratic equation)
if the curves dont intersect they dont have a solution, So, D < 0
Now D = 4a2 + 12(a - 6) < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + 3a - 18 < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a + 6)(a - 3) < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a + 6) (a - 3) < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\forall{/tex} a {tex}\in{/tex}(-6, 3) curves (i) and (ii) do not intersect.

= {tex}=4 \sqrt{1-2|\cos \theta||\sin \theta|}=4 \sqrt{1-|\sin 2 \theta|}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Range = [0, 4]

questions.question: If the equation $\frac{p}{x-p}+\frac{q}{x-q}$ = 1 has two roots equal in magnitude but opposite in sign then the value of 2p + q is:
questions.question_raw: If the equation {tex}\frac{p}{x-p}+\frac{q}{x-q}{/tex} = 1 has two roots equal in magnitude but opposite in sign then the value of 2p + q is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{p}{x-p}+\frac{q}{x-q}=1{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (x -q) + q(x - p) = (x - p) (x - q)
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x2 - (2p + 2q)x + 3pq = 0
If {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots then {tex}\alpha{/tex} = -{tex}\beta{/tex} (Given)
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\beta{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2p + 2q = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} p + q = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2p + q = p

questions.question: If |z_1| = 1, |z_2| = 2, |z_3| = 3 and |9z_1z_2 + 4z_1z_3 + z_2z_3| = 12, then the value of |z_1 + z_2 + z_3| is
questions.question_raw: If |z1| = 1, |z2| = 2, |z3| = 3 and |9z1z2 + 4z1z3 + z2z3| = 12, then the value of |z1 + z2 + z3| is
questions.explanation:
|z1| = 1, |z2| = 2, |z3| = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z1{tex}\bar z_1{/tex} = 1, z2{tex}\bar z_2{/tex} = 4, z3{tex}\bar z_3{/tex} = 9 ...[{tex}\because{/tex} z{tex}\bar z{/tex} = |z|2]
Given, |9z1z2 + 4z1z3 + z2z3| = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |z3{tex}\bar z_3{/tex}z1z2 + z2{tex}\bar z_2{/tex}z1z3 + z1{tex}\bar z_1{/tex}z2z3| = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |z1z2z3| {tex}\left|\bar{z}_{3}+\bar{z}_{2}+\bar{z}_{1}\right|{/tex} = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |z1 + z2 + z3| = 2

questions.question: Let f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c, f(-2) = 0 and $\frac{-b}{2 a}$ = 1, then the roots of f(x) = 0 are:
questions.question_raw: Let f(x) = ax2 + bx + c, f(-2) = 0 and {tex}\frac{-b}{2 a}{/tex} = 1, then the roots of f(x) = 0 are:
questions.explanation:
f(-2 ) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (-2)2 a + b (-2) + c = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 4a - 2b + c = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}1-\frac{b}{2 a}+\frac{c}{4 a}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 1 + 1 + {tex}\frac{c}{4 a}{/tex} = 0 ... [Given {tex}\frac{-b}{2 a}{/tex} = 1]
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{c}{4 a}{/tex} = -2

f(x) = 0

questions.question:
If cos$\theta$ + i sin$\theta$ is a solution to the equation a_0x^4 + a_1x^3 + a_2x^2 + a_3x + a_4 = 0, then consider the statements given below.
 a_0 sin 4$\theta$ + a_1 sin 3$\theta$ + a_2 sin 2$\theta$ + a_3 sin$\theta$ + a_4 = 0
 a_1 sin$\theta$ + a_2 sin 2$\theta$ + a_3 sin 3$\theta$ + a_4 sin$\theta$ = 0
questions.question_raw:
If cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex} is a solution to the equation a0x4 + a1x3 + a2x2 + a3x + a4 = 0, then consider the statements given below.

  1. a0 sin 4{tex}\theta{/tex} + a1 sin 3{tex}\theta{/tex} + a2 sin 2{tex}\theta{/tex} + a3 sin{tex}\theta{/tex} + a4 = 0
  2. a1 sin{tex}\theta{/tex} + a2 sin 2{tex}\theta{/tex} + a3 sin 3{tex}\theta{/tex} + a4 sin{tex}\theta{/tex} = 0
questions.explanation:
cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex} is root of a0x4 + a1x3 + a2x2 + a3x + a4 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a0 (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex})4 + a1 (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex})3 + a2 (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex})2 + a3 (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a4 = 0 ...(i)
a0 (cos4{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin4{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a1 (cos3{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin3{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a2 (cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin2{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a3 (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a4 = 0
Equating imaginary part to zero, we get a0 sin 4{tex}\theta{/tex} + a1 sin 3{tex}\theta{/tex} + a2 sin 2{tex}\theta{/tex} + a3 sin{tex}\theta{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Statement (i) is false
{tex}\because{/tex} cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 for any {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\in{/tex} R
Dividing equation (i) by (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex})4 on both sides
a0 + {tex}\frac{a_{1}}{(\cos \theta+i \sin \theta)^{2}}+\frac{a_{2}}{(\cos \theta+i \sin \theta)^{2}}+\frac{a_{3}}{(\cos \theta+i \sin \theta)^{3}}+\frac{a_{1}}{(\cos \theta+i \sin \theta)^{4}}{/tex} = 0
a0 + a1 (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} - i sin{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a2 (cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} - i sin2{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a3 (cos3{tex}\theta{/tex} - i sin3{tex}\theta{/tex}) + a4 (cos4{tex}\theta{/tex} - i sin4{tex}\theta{/tex}) = 0
Equating imaginary part to zero,
a1 sin{tex}\theta{/tex} + a2 sin2{tex}\theta{/tex} + a3 sin3{tex}\theta{/tex} + a4 sin4{tex}\theta{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Statement (ii) is true.

questions.question: If one root of (x - 1) (7 - x) = m is thrice the other, then the value of m is:

(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 2
(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(2)2 = m + 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = 5

questions.question: If the roots of the quadratic equation $\frac{x-m}{m x+1}=\frac{x+n}{n x+1}$ are reciprocal to each other, then:
questions.question_raw: If the roots of the quadratic equation {tex}\frac{x-m}{m x+1}=\frac{x+n}{n x+1}{/tex} are reciprocal to each other, then:
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\frac{x-m}{m x+1}=\frac{x+n}{n x+1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 (m - n) + 2mnx + (m + n) = 0
Let the roots be {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{\alpha}{/tex}. Then,
{tex}\alpha \cdot \frac{1}{\alpha}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{m+n}{m-n}{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} m - n = m + n
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n = 0

1 + i

questions.question: Let z be a complex number satisfying 2 $\leq$ |z| $\leq$ 4 and a $\leq$ $\left|z+\frac{1}{z}\right|$ $\leq$ b then which of the following is not correct:

options.text: If z = $r e^{i \theta}$ then $\left|z+\frac{1}{z}\right|^{2}$ = r^2 + $\frac{1}{r^{2}}$ + 2 cos2$\theta$

questions.explanation:
z = r (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} + i sin{tex}\theta{/tex}) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{z}=\frac{1}{r(\cos \theta+i \sin \theta)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{r}\left(\frac{\cos \theta-i \sin \theta}{\cos ^{2} \theta+\sin ^{2} \theta}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{r}{/tex} (cos{tex}\theta{/tex} - i sin{tex}\theta{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow\left|z+\frac{1}{z}\right|^{2}=\left(r+\frac{1}{r}\right)^{2}{/tex} cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} + {tex}\left(r-\frac{1}{r}\right)^{2}{/tex} sin2{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}=\left(r^{2}+\frac{1}{r^{2}}\right){/tex} (cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} + sin2{tex}\theta{/tex}) + 2 (cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} - sin2{tex}\theta{/tex})
= r2 + {tex}\frac{1}{r^{2}}{/tex} + 2 cos2{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left|z+\frac{1}{z}\right|_{\min }^{2}{/tex} = 4 + {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} + 2(-1) = {tex}\frac{9}{4} \Rightarrow{/tex} a = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\left|z+\frac{1}{z}\right|_{max}^{2}{/tex} = 16 + {tex}\frac{1}{16}{/tex} + 2(1) = {tex}\frac{289}{16} \Rightarrow{/tex} b = {tex}\frac{17}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b = {tex}\frac{3}{2}+\frac{17}{4}=\frac{23}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0 (a $\neq$ 0; a, b, c being different), then (1 + $\alpha$ + $\alpha^2$) (1 + $\beta$ + $\beta^2$) is:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots of the equation ax2 + bx + c = 0 (a {tex}\neq{/tex} 0; a, b, c being different), then (1 + {tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\alpha^2{/tex}) (1 + {tex}\beta{/tex} + {tex}\beta^2{/tex}) is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\alpha+\beta=-\frac{b}{a} {/tex} and {tex} \alpha \beta=\frac{c}{a}{/tex}
{tex}\left(1+\alpha+\alpha^{2}\right)\left(1+\beta+\beta^{2}\right){/tex}
= {tex}1+(\alpha+\beta)+\left(\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}\right)+\alpha \beta+\alpha \beta(\alpha+\beta)+\alpha^{2} \beta^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}1+(\alpha+\beta)+(\alpha+\beta)^{2}-\alpha \beta+\alpha \beta(\alpha+\beta)+(\alpha \beta)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}1-\frac{b}{a}+\frac{b^{2}}{a^{2}}-\frac{c}{a}+\left(\frac{c}{a}\right)\left(-\frac{b}{a}\right)+\frac{c^{2}}{a^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(a^{2}+b^{2}+c^{2}-a b-b c-c a\right)}{a^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left[(a-b)^{2}+(b-c)^{2}+(c-a)^{2}\right]}{2 a^{2}}{/tex} > 0

questions.question: If z^2 + z + 1 = 0, where z is a complex number, then the value of $\left(z+\frac{1}{z}\right)^{2}+\left(z^{2}+\frac{1}{z^{2}}\right)^{2}+\left(z^{3}+\frac{1}{z^{3}}\right)^{2}+\ldots+\left(z^{6}+\frac{1}{z^{6}}\right)^{2}$ is
questions.question_raw: If z2 + z + 1 = 0, where z is a complex number, then the value of {tex}\left(z+\frac{1}{z}\right)^{2}+\left(z^{2}+\frac{1}{z^{2}}\right)^{2}+\left(z^{3}+\frac{1}{z^{3}}\right)^{2}+\ldots+\left(z^{6}+\frac{1}{z^{6}}\right)^{2}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
z2 + z + 1 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = {tex}\frac{-1+\sqrt{3} i}{2}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = {tex}\omega{/tex} or {tex}\omega^2{/tex}
Consider, z = {tex}\omega{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{z}=\frac{1}{\omega}=\omega^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \left(z+\frac{1}{z}\right)^{2}+\left(z^{2}+\frac{1}{z^{2}}\right)^{2}+\ldots+\left(z^{6}+\frac{1}{z^{6}}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\omega+\omega^{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\omega^{2}+\omega^{4}\right)^{2}+\left(\omega^{3}+\omega^{6}\right)^{2}{/tex}{tex}+\left(\omega^{4}+\omega^{8}\right)^{2}+\left(\omega^{5}+\omega^{10}\right)^{2}+\left(\omega^{6}+\omega^{12}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= (-1)2 + {tex}\left(\omega^{2}+\omega\right)^{2}{/tex} + (1 + 1)2 + {tex}\left(\omega+\omega^{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\omega^{2}+\omega\right)^{2}{/tex} + (1 + 1)2
= 1 + (-1)2 + 4 + (-1)2 + (-1)2 + 4
= 12

questions.question: If $\alpha, \beta \in$ C are the distinct roots of the equation x^2 - x + 1 = 0, then $\alpha^{101}+\beta^{107}$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha, \beta \in{/tex} C are the distinct roots of the equation x2 - x + 1 = 0, then {tex}\alpha^{101}+\beta^{107}{/tex} is equal to

{tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the roots of the given equation

{tex}\beta=\frac{1-\sqrt{3} i}{2}=-\omega^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\alpha^{101}+\beta^{107}=(-\omega)^{101}+\left(-\omega^{2}\right)^{107}{/tex}
{tex}=-\left[\left(\omega^{3}\right)^{33} \cdot \omega^{2}+\left(\omega^{3}\right)^{71} \cdot \omega\right]{/tex}

questions.question: If the difference between the roots of the equation x^2 + ax + 1 = 0 is less than $\sqrt{5}$, then the set of possible values of a is:
questions.question_raw: If the difference between the roots of the equation x2 + ax + 1 = 0 is less than {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex}, then the set of possible values of a is:

{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} a2 < 9

questions.question: If z and $\omega$ are two non-zero complex numbers such that |z$\omega$| = 1 and arg(z) - arg($\omega$) = $\frac{\pi}{2}$, then $\bar{z} \omega$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If z and {tex}\omega{/tex} are two non-zero complex numbers such that |z{tex}\omega{/tex}| = 1 and arg(z) - arg({tex}\omega{/tex}) = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}, then {tex}\bar{z} \omega{/tex} is equal to
